rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF LIAN ANAIS JEONG ENGLE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR TERMINATION OF GUARDIANSHIP of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues to be cleared: (1) Proof of service of Notice of Hearing [DE-121] with a copy of the petition and having been mailed to all persons who are entitled to notice must be filed (Prob. Code sec. 2621 It is insufficient for the petition to allege that a notice was sent. (2) The notice of hearing must include the Notice of Directions for Remote Appearances and Access. Local Rule 14.1.C. 6). (3) Need further explanation of paragraph #8 stating that the bank account was "converted" to the former minor's name. A blocked account cannot be converted in such a way. Need clarification on whether the account was a custodian account, not a blocked account despite the court order. (4) Need bank statements up to the current - from August 2023 to 4/30/2024. (5) Guardianship of Estate has been "terminated" by operation of law when Lian attained majority. Prob. Code 1600(a), The court was expecting a petition settling the final report. Local Rule 14.46.D.2). (6) Exhibit "A" and "B" are not attached to the petition. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF KEVIN KA-CHEUNG YU
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/13/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service has been filed. (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) Proof of payment of the assessment fee must be filed before an accounting can be approved. (4) Original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (5) For a first accounting, the conservator shall provide to the court all account statements showing the account balance immediately preceding the date the conservator was appointed. [Probate Code Section 2620(c)(2).] (6) Additional quotes mentioned in paragraph 15 have not yet been filed. (It is unclear from the documents attached as Exhibit B how the two Allied Restoration quotes in the summary were determined.) (7) Paragraph 20 is unclear as to how the total of the market value of assets on hand plus the requested loan was determined. Fees will not be ordered to be paid without services having been provided and detailed time sheets having been filed to support any request for fees. [Probate Code Sections 2641(a) and 2642(a).] If the conservator intended to seek fees on account, that must be clearly set forth. [Probate Code Section 2634.1 and CRC 7.755.] (8) Fee declarations have not yet been filed, as alleged in paragraphs 22 and 23. (9) There is no schedule of liabilities in the accounting or current statement from the lender, confirming the reverse mortgage liability. Deadline is 5/3/24 for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a probate referee HAS BEEN appointed.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD FRANCISCO BASANES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/6/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service has been filed. (2) Notice must also have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) The report does not fully meet the requirements of LRSF 14.35 J. Deadline is 4/24/24 for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] All pleadings must also show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" or "Respondent" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.])

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Approval Of Release And Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The procedural issues to be cleared or addressed: (1) Need allegation under which specific provision of the Power of Attorney of Anita Murillo Patricia is authorized to sign the settlement agreement on behalf of Anita. The previous petition(s) has been reportedly signed by Anita herself. (2) Clarification as to whom Ms. Brown represents. She did Anita in the initial petition; however, she appears to have been representing Carla (the SNT trustee), as well as Anita (the SNT beneficiary), now possibly Patricia (alleged attorney-in-fact for Anita), too. (3) No proposed order has been submitted. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CECILIA P BLAKE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/13/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service of notice of this hearing has been filed. (3) Notice must also have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) No waiver of accounting has been filed yet by the other heir, as alleged in paragraph 15 of the petition. (5) Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary HAS PREVIOUSLY BEEN confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. (6) Paragraph 10 of the petition claims that the decedent owned "no property" but assets were inventoried and are on hand for distribution. (7) Paragraph 13 of the petition states that income taxes will be paid before distribution. Are there taxes due? They must be reported and paid before distribution. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/3/24. Courtesy copies to be provided would be: (1) the proof of service of notice; (2) the waiver of account by the other heir; (3) a verified supplement to the petition fully addressing Probate Code Section 9202(b) and clarifying the allegations regarding Decedent's ownership of property and the status of any individual or estate income taxes due. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The statutory compensation would have been $2,050.32, based on the I&A. Probate Code Section 10800(a)(1) means four percent on the first $100,000, meaning up to that amount.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DELLA WALKER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/13/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] It appears, however, that the executor is the only beneficiary of the devisee trust, of which she is also the trustee, so that notice may not be required. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/3/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ZOILA REGINA JEREZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/29/2024 (no earlier date available). The following issues must be addressed: 1) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 15 day before the hearing. 2) Notice must be given to the person who has requested special notice. Copies of the petition and supplemental papers must also be served. 3) All the information required by CRC 7.250(a) must be provided as to each reportable IAEA action taken by the administrator. 4) No court approval has been sought for payment of fees to the landlord-tenant counsel (if such fees were paid out of the estate funds). LRSF 14.50. 5) All the information required by LRSF 14.35(J)(1) must be provided, including assignments or powers of attorney that may affect distribution. 6) The petition must be amended and renoticed to allege a specific period for which the administration is sought to be continued. LRSF 14.35(J)(2).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF APRIL WILLIAMS FUNKHOUSER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/29/2024 per counsel's request (via e-mail on 04/02/2024). The following issues remain unaddressed: 1) An amended DE-147 form must be filed to list the proposed personal representative's telephone number at item 2 of the acknowledgment of receipt section. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-121 form at least 15 days before the hearing, absent signed waivers. 3) Does the proposed administrator intend to represent herself once appointed? 4) A nomination signed by Reginald Lawrence II who has priority must be filed. Note: If Reginald Lawrence II's nomination cannot be obtained, notice must be given to the San Francisco Public Administrator having higher priority than the petitioner.
